title: Enron
compiled_truth: Enron Corporation was an American energy company headquartered in Houston, Texas. Founded in 1985 through the merger of InterNorth and Houston Natural Gas, Enron grew from a natural gas pipeline company into the largest energy trading company in the United States. Its collapse in 2001 was the largest corporate bankruptcy in US history at the time, triggering criminal investigations that resulted in convictions of senior executives including Jeffrey Skilling and Andrew Fastow. The email corpus covers approximately 150 Enron employees across trading, legal, regulatory, and executive functions. Total email volume: 1,481,881.
timeline:
- **1985-07-01** | InterNorth + Houston Natural Gas merge to form Enron
- **1989-07-01** | Enron begins natural gas trading
- **1999-11-29** | Enron Online launches
- **2000-12-31** | Enron reports $100B in annual revenues
- **2001-10-16** | Announces $618M Q3 loss; SEC investigation begins
- **2001-12-02** | Files Chapter 11 bankruptcy ($63.4B in assets)
